name: Chicken Soup with Greens and Shiitake Mushroo
recipeIngredient: 2-inch piece fresh ginger, cut into coins
1/2 lb fresh Shiitake mushrooms or oyster mushrooms
1/2 lb Napa cabbage or other green cabbage, thinly sliced with stems removed
1 1/2 teaspoons hot pepper sesame oil (see note)
Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the skin from the chicken and place it in a soup pot.
2. Add the stock, wine, ginger, and onions. Bring to a simmer.
3. Simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through. Skim off any scum and fat that rises to the surface. Do not boil; otherwise, the stock will become cloudy.
4. Remove stems from Shiitake mushrooms and add them to the stock. Slice the mushroom caps thickly and sauté in drops of olive oil until lightly browned. Set aside.
5. Remove the chicken from the pot and cut the meat into long slivers, discarding the bones.
6. Strain the stock carefully, discarding ginger, onions, and Shiitake stems. Wash out the pot and add the cabbage. Cook for 2 minutes.
7. Add the chicken, spinach, and sesame oil. Cook for one minute more. Correct seasoning with salt and pepper and serve immediately.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings
Note: Hot pepper sesame oil is optional and can be adjusted according to personal preference.
